surface.name: Send Linux host metrics to a Grafana Cloud dashboard
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Send Linux host metrics to a Grafana Cloud dashboard. Grafana Cloud presents multiple data routes, but its official integration quickstart supplies a complete Linux path from account and host prerequisites through Grafana Alloy installation to visible host metrics in a preconfigured dashboard. Prior surface before policy commit: Grafana Cloud data source and telemetry route not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: Linux server integration
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This complete example assumes a Linux host and administrator access, while the same page says macOS and Windows integrations are similar.

alternatives_considered.name: Externally managed data source
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This connects an existing data source and creates a dashboard without sending data into Grafana-managed storage.

alternatives_considered.name: Demo data
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This avoids a real telemetry source and represents a different learning outcome.

alternatives_considered.name: Existing observability deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Prometheus remote_write, manual Alloy, and other forwarding methods differ from the recommended integration candidate.

entry_point.developer_intent: View a first real telemetry signal in Grafana Cloud without a preselected signal type or source.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No Grafana Cloud account, stack, data source, telemetry type, collector, or monitored system is selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The Grafana Cloud Linux integration quickstart ends when recent metrics for the configured host are visible in the preconfigured Nodes dashboard.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Recent metrics for the named host are visible in the dashboard.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Recent metrics for the named host are visible in the dashboard.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Recent metrics for the named host are visible in the dashboard.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A free Grafana Cloud account and stack are required. Account creation starts a 14-day unlimited-data and unlimited-user trial; after it ends, the account transitions to the usage-restricted free-forever plan.
Select externally connected data or sent data, then the signal type and source.
The Linux integration candidate requires a Linux machine to monitor.
Terminal and administrator access to the monitored machine plus outbound HTTPS access on port 443 to `*.grafana.net` are required for the candidate.

primary_path.action: Create the free Grafana Cloud account and stack, then select demo data, connect externally managed data, or send real telemetry into Grafana Cloud.
details: Account creation begins a 14-day unlimited trial and later transitions to the restricted free-forever plan.
Demo data avoids instrumenting a real system, connecting external data queries that source, and sending telemetry stores it in Grafana Cloud.
primary_path.success_signal: No common first-data signal exists at this choice point.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account signup and stack creation precede all branches.

primary_path.action: Choose telemetry signal, source, and integration or collector method.
details: Metrics can use integrations, Prometheus remote_write, manual Alloy, or other methods.
The complete Linux integration candidate installs Alloy and supplies a preconfigured dashboard.
primary_path.input: Human-selected source and signal plus its access requirements.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected path can terminate with data visible in Grafana.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Credentials, agents, firewall rules, configuration, scraping delay, dashboard, and errors vary by route.

primary_path.action: Create or sign into a free Grafana Cloud account and open its Grafana Cloud stack.
details: New accounts begin with a 14-day unlimited-data and unlimited-user trial and later transition to the restricted free-forever plan.
primary_path.input: Account identity and Grafana Cloud signup details.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Signup, sign-in, or stack-provisioning failure prevents access to Connections.

primary_path.action: Prepare the Linux machine with terminal and administrator access, and configure its firewall to allow outbound HTTPS on port 443 to `*.grafana.net`.
details: The firewall rule is an explicit prerequisite in the official Linux integration guide.
primary_path.input: Linux host, administrator privileges, terminal, and control of its outbound firewall rules.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can administer the host and its firewall permits outbound HTTPS on port 443 to `*.grafana.net`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing host access, privileges, or a firewall rule permitting outbound HTTPS on port 443 to `*.grafana.net` blocks Alloy installation or telemetry delivery.

primary_path.action: In the Grafana Cloud stack, open Connections, search for Linux, select the Linux integration tile, and open Configuration details.
primary_path.input: Authenticated stack and Linux integration selection.
primary_path.success_signal: Configuration details for the Linux integration are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Insufficient stack access or failure to find the integration stops configuration.

primary_path.action: Choose and enter the hostname label that will identify this machine in Grafana Cloud.
details: The label is required by the integration and is used to distinguish the host in dashboards.
primary_path.success_signal: The configuration accepts the hostname value.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or unsuitable hostname prevents the generated configuration from representing the host correctly.

primary_path.action: Keep the reduced metric set or open Advanced integration and choose the full metric set.
details: The official flow allows either reduced or full metrics before generating the collector configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: The desired metric option is reflected in the generated setup.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting a different metric set changes what the first dashboard can display.

primary_path.action: Copy and run the generated commands on the Linux machine to install, configure, and start Grafana Alloy.
details: The generated configuration sends the selected Linux metrics to the current Grafana Cloud stack.
primary_path.input: Generated integration commands and administrator access.
primary_path.success_signal: The installation and service-start commands finish successfully.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package, privilege, configuration, service, credential, or network errors prevent collection.

primary_path.action: Open Dashboards and select the Nodes dashboard in the Linux Node folder.
primary_path.input: Running Alloy integration and Grafana Cloud stack.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The dashboard can initially be empty before the first scrape arrives.

primary_path.action: Wait for Grafana Alloy to complete its first scrape and send the host metrics.
details: The integration guide explicitly notes that the dashboard is initially empty.
primary_path.input: Running collector and open Nodes dashboard.
primary_path.output: First Linux telemetry batch in Grafana Cloud.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A prolonged empty dashboard indicates a collector, configuration, connectivity, or scrape problem.

primary_path.action: Verify that the Nodes dashboard displays recent metrics for the configured Linux host.
details: The preconfigured dashboard exposes host signals such as CPU, load, memory, disk, and network metrics according to the selected metric set.
primary_path.success_signal: Recent metrics for the named host are visible in the dashboard.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing host series or empty panels mean the documented first success has not been reached.

branches.condition: The developer wants the fastest populated-dashboard experience without instrumenting a real system.
branches.path: Sign in to the Grafana Cloud stack, open Apps, Demo Data Dashboards, select the SRE or Weather demo, and select Install Dashboards. Then open the folder named for the demo and inspect its populated dashboards and hosted data sources.
branches.effect_on_first_success: A populated dashboard is visible with no collector, source credentials, host access, or billing, but it proves dashboard exploration rather than telemetry from the developer's own system.

branches.condition: The current user cannot create data sources and dashboards.
branches.path: Ask a user with the built-in Administrator role or equivalent create-data-source and create-dashboard permissions to install the demo, then return to the demo folder.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The no-instrumentation path adds an administrator permission gate.

branches.path: Use a Grafana Cloud account and Linux machine with terminal, admin, and outbound HTTPS access; in the stack open Connections, search for Linux, open its tile, follow Configuration details, choose and enter the required hostname label, choose reduced or full metrics with Advanced integration, install and start Grafana Alloy using the generated configuration, open Dashboards, select the Nodes dashboard in the Linux Node folder, wait through the first scrape while it is initially empty, and verify host metrics appear.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The preconfigured dashboard displays recent Linux host metrics after the collector's first scrape.

branches.path: Add the supported data source and credentials, test the connection, then create or import a dashboard that queries that external source.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Grafana visualizes data without storing that source in Grafana Cloud's managed telemetry backends.

branches.condition: An existing Prometheus or observability deployment is selected.
branches.path: Use Prometheus remote_write, manual Grafana Alloy, or another supported forwarding method instead of the Linux integration installer.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Collector configuration and dashboard selection are determined by the existing deployment.

friction_gates.description: A Grafana Cloud account and stack must exist; the account starts with a 14-day unlimited trial and then transitions to the restricted free-forever plan.
Installing Demo Data Dashboards requires permission to create data sources and dashboards, such as the built-in Administrator role.
Externally connect versus send, signal type, data source, and collection route must be selected.
Linux integration needs command-line and administrator access to the monitored host.
The monitored host firewall must allow outbound HTTPS to Grafana Cloud.
Hostname label and reduced versus full metric set must be selected and inserted into Alloy configuration.
The dashboard remains empty until the collector reaches its first scrape interval, then fills over time.

sources.title: Get started with monitoring using an integration
evidence_supported: Initial empty dashboard and first-scrape wait

sources.title: Send metrics to Grafana Cloud
sections_used: Send data from an existing observability deployment

sources.title: Create a Grafana Cloud account
evidence_supported: Cloud Account Portal and next-route choice

sources.title: Work with demo data in Grafana Cloud
sections_used: Install demo data sources and dashboards
evidence_supported: Apps and Demo Data Dashboards navigation
No billing for hosted demo data
Populated SRE and Weather dashboard outcomes

uncertainties.question: Which data architecture, telemetry type, and source should represent Grafana Cloud first success?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes account stack pages, credentials, collector installation, firewall, configuration, wait states, and dashboard.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Grafana Cloud's official Get started page explicitly begins with multiple connect and send options.

excluded_after_success: No common after-success scope is selected while signal and source remain unresolved.
Alerting, dashboards beyond the preconfigured view, logs, traces, profiles, SLOs, synthetic monitoring, cost optimization, and production collector management are excluded.
